summary: The title promises 'First-Principles' but the method is a GNN. That's a contradiction you can't ignore.
body: Look, you can't call it 'First-Principles Calculations' if you're just fitting a Graph Neural Network. The title says one thing, but the summary admits you're using a 'GNN model' to predict the moduli. That's not first-principles; that's curve-fitting. You're not solving the Schrödinger equation for the electrons, you're just interpolating based on what you've seen before. If you want to predict elastic moduli, go back to the Hamiltonian and the electron density. Otherwise, change the title to reflect what you're actually doing. Don't fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
